TextENGNew: ``...I don't want to even look at anything sweet for at least three days...@k@vS09/01/170100121. Ughhh...``
``Ahahahaha, you overdid it, Keiichi-kun.``
I couldn't understand how Rena could be so calm when she'd been scarfing things down just as fast as I had...
``Girls must have like four stomachs or something...@k@vS09/01/170100123. There's definitely at least one extra in there just for dessert...``
``My! What a dreadful thing to say to a lady!!@k@vS09/04/170400019. For such insolence, you shall receive this!``
Squish.
Satoko jabbed her finger into my swollen belly.
``Blegh!!@k@vS09/01/170100125. S-Stop it!@k@vS09/01/170100126. Don't press on my stomach!!@k@vS09/01/170100127. Everything's going to come spilling out!!``
Hearing that, Satoko and Rika-chan broke into wicked grins.@k ...A chill ran down my spine.
Why were Satoko and Rika-chan grinning like that, wiggling their fingers as they crept toward me?
``Waaaah, stop it you two, don't you have any blood or tears?!``
``Ohohohoho!!@k@vS09/04/170400021. Did you really think I'd let a once-in-a-lifetime chance like this slip away?!``
``Keiichi's puffy tummy is in big trouble~``
``Ahahahahaha, Keiichi-kun's jiggly-wiggly tummy is sooo kyute~☆``
``S-Stop it, you guys...!!``
``Wah, aaaaagh!!!``
Before I knew it, Rena had joined Satoko and Rika-chan in their mischief, and we were still carrying on even after spilling out in front of Angel Mort.
While I desperately tried to shield my stomach, someone snuck up and got me from behind.
``Owaaaah!!@k@vS09/01/170100132. Wh-Who was that?!``
``Helloooo~.@k@vS09/06/170600002. Good afternoon, Kei-chan and all his merry friends!``
``Oh, it's Shii-chan!@k@vS09/02/170200131. Hi there!``
``Wait, you weren't at the restaurant today, were you?``
``I'm on the night shift today.@k@vS09/06/170600004. I just got here and changed.``
``By the way... how can you just walk outside the restaurant dressed like that?``
``It's not a big deal.@k@vS09/06/170600006. It's nowhere near as embarrassing as a certain someone who paraded through the village in an absolutely shameless outfit.``
...Stab!!
...Ughhh... I was just starting to forget about that...!@k I can never get married now...
``Anyway, I hear you all really tore the place apart today?``
``Ahahahahaha, well, yeah~.@k@vS09/02/170200134. Once it turned into a club activity... you know how it goes!``
``You made quite the mess in there...@k@vS09/06/170600009. Sis felt responsible, so she's in there helping the staff clean up.``
``...Don't you think we should have stayed to help clean?``
``Oh, don't worry about that.@k@vS09/06/170600011. We can't very well make our customers do the cleaning.``
``...Besides, Mii made the biggest mess of all, I think.``
We all nodded in agreement.@k Everyone nodded together...
``Oh, and Sis asked me to tell you all.@k@vS09/06/170600013. She says you can go on home ahead of her.``
``No, we'll wait.@k@vS09/02/170200136. Right, everyone?``
Rena said it as if it were the most natural thing in the world.
changeReason: Old says "asked for our agreement" (request); new says "said it as if most natural thing" (assertion) -- different speech act.

TextENGNew: And it was natural, for all of us.@k Satoko, Rika-chan, and I all nodded without hesitation.
``If we can't help her clean, the least we can do is wait.@k@vS09/01/170100135. If we just left after she told us to go ahead, I think she'd actually feel lonely.``
``Ohh...@k@vS09/06/170600015. Kei-chan, you've grown up a little after all, haven't you~``
``Ahahahahaha, well of course!@k@vS09/02/170200138. That's because we've been training him properly.@k@vS09/02/170200139. Riight~~?``
``Indeed!``
``Nipah~☆``
``Wh-What's with you girls always ganging up on me at times like this!``
Girls are so unfair. How can they band together so easily just because they're the same gender?!
In the end, we dragged Shion into the commotion too, and the racket started all over again.
``Shion, how long are you going to keep slacking off?!@k@vS09/03/170300043. Where did you put the rags?!``
``Oh dear.@k@vS09/06/170600017. Well then, I'd better get back.``
``Mii-chaaaan!@k@vS09/02/170200141. Is the cleaning going to take much longer?``
``Huh?@k@vS09/03/170300045. What are you guys still doing here?``
``You're waiting for this old man?!@k@vS09/03/170300047. You don't have to worry about me!@k@vS09/03/170300048. Geez, honestly!``
She looked absolutely delighted, yet the moment she opened her mouth, it was all bluster.
Would it kill her to just honestly say thank you for once?
``Idiot, we're friends, aren't we?@k@vS09/01/170100138. We'll wait till you're done.``
``I wish we could help too...@k@vS09/02/170200143. Sorry.``

``I told you, it's fine!@k@vS09/03/170300050. Listen, I'm going to wait for Shion's shift to end tonight, then head to our house in Okinomiya for a change.@k@vS09/03/170300051. So really, don't worry about it.@k@vS09/03/170300052. The thought alone is enough. Thanks.``
``...Oh, are you sure?@k@vS09/04/170400025. I do feel rather bad about leaving.``
``Yeah.@k@vS09/02/170200145. I kind of feel bad too, you know?``
``There are plenty of people helping, so it's fine.@k@vS09/03/170300054. Get back to Hinamizawa before the wind turns cold.``
``...Understood.``
``Mii, good luck with the cleaning. Fight-o, yay!``
``Kei-chan,@k@vS09/03/170300058. I'm entrusting you to escort these young ladies home safely.@k@vS09/03/170300059. Make sure to guard them from any assassins along the way.``
Where exactly were these ladies who needed escorting?@k ...If anything, I was the one who needed a bodyguard.
Still, at least all five of us got to see each other one last time, so we could say proper goodbyes and end the day right.
``Byeee, Mii-chaaaan!@k@vS09/02/170200147. See you tomorrow!``
``Yeah!@k@vS09/03/170300061. See you tomorrow!``
``All right, let's get going!``
We waved at Mion until she disappeared back into the restaurant, then hopped on our bicycles and left Okinomiya behind.
On the way home, everyone was still buzzing about the day's mayhem, the excitement far from fading.
From the eating contest to the nightmarish penalty game, and then the absolutely unprecedented event that swept up every customer in the restaurant... everything had been pure, glorious chaos.
The waitresses had been flustered at first, but gradually they seemed to start enjoying themselves, and by the end the whole restaurant was caught up in the revelry.
``It really was something!!@k@vS09/04/170400027. And Tomita-san and Okamura-san during all that—they were absolutely hilarious!``
``...In the end, Kameda and Irie both ended up in a pitiful state too, poor things.``
changeReason: Old says "I felt sorry" (narrator's feeling); new says "ended up in a pitiful state" (their condition) -- different focus.

TextENGNew: ``Be that as it may!@k@vS09/01/170100141. My speech was pretty impressive too, right?!@k@vS09/01/170100142. The whole restaurant was on my side after that!!``
``...If Keiichi had been born in a different era and a different country, he might have become someone truly extraordinary.``
``This smooth talker?!@k@vS09/04/170400029. No matter where or when he was born, he'd never amount to anything decent!``
``...If he'd been born just a hundred years earlier, his face might have ended up on banknotes.``
``Money with Keiichi-san's face on it? How pathetic!@k@vS09/04/170400031. They'd only be worth two or three hundred yen at most!``
Satoko and Rika-chan chattered on and on, giggling all the while.
I was a little worn out from all the excitement, so I just listened quietly to their conversation.
Before I knew it, the breeze was growing cooler.@k Our shadows had started stretching longer without my noticing.
Satoko and Rika-chan still seemed to have energy to spare... but what about Rena?
She'd been quiet for a while now.
Maybe she had a stomachache from eating too much?@k I glanced over at her.
But that worry was unfounded.
Rena looked... how should I put it?@k ...Deeply, completely content.
As though the day had been so much fun, she simply couldn't contain it.
As though she were so happy, so blissfully happy, that nothing else mattered.
...Those feelings radiated from her expression without a single word being spoken.
Seeing that look on Rena's face made me realize, belatedly, that today couldn't simply be summed up by the word "fun."
``...Today was really, truly wonderful, wasn't it?``
``Mm.@k@vS09/02/170200149. ...What about you, Keiichi-kun?``
``I had an amazing time too!@k@vS09/01/170100145. I wish every day could be like today—tomorrow, the day after, all of them.``
``I feel the same way.@k@vS09/02/170200151. I wished that the time we spent in the restaurant today could last forever.``
``Heh, that's pretty poetic of you.``
``Didn't you feel that way too, Keiichi-kun?``
Rena didn't even notice I was teasing her and answered in complete earnest.
Maybe that was a tactless thing to say.@k I felt a pang of regret.
``No, I totally agree.``
``But you know, Keiichi-kun.@k@vS09/02/170200154. It's not as if today is the only happy day.``
A cool breeze lifted her hair gently.
Rena smiled at me with a surprisingly mature expression.@k ...My heart skipped a beat.
``Every single one of our days here in Hinamizawa is irreplaceable, precious, and beautiful—a truly happy day.``
``Even the boring days when club activities get cancelled.``
``...You really have a mature way of looking at things, Rena.``
``Ahahahaha, not really.@k@vS09/02/170200158. Honestly, I still haven't had my fill of fun yet.@k@vS09/02/170200159. Maybe the excitement just hasn't worn off.``
Rena let out a soft laugh, and I found myself laughing along with her.
Satoko apparently thought we were laughing about her, and she started giving me grief about it.
After that, it was back to the usual noisy fun.
``...Well then, Satoko and I shall part ways here.``
``Rika, can't we walk with them a bit further and turn at the tobacco shop instead?``
``...Did you forget that our nightlight burnt out?@k@vS09/05/170500023. Without it, it'll be pitch dark when we go to sleep.``
``Th-That... would indeed be a problem.``
``Oh, Makino-san's general store?@k@vS09/02/170200161. Then this is where we say goodbye.@k@vS09/02/170200162. If you go any further, you'll end up taking a big detour.``
A general store...?@k ...Oh right, I remember now.
If they were stopping there before heading back to the shrine, this was definitely the right place to turn off.
``I see.@k@vS09/01/170100150. Well, see you later, Rika-chan, Satoko.@k@vS09/01/170100151. See you tomorrow.``
``...See you tomorrow.``
``Farewell then!@k@vS09/04/170400035. Keiichi-san, Rena-san!``
``Mm, bye-bye you two!@k@vS09/02/170200164. Bye-byeee!``
Rika-chan and Satoko waved again and again, then dashed off down the narrow path cutting through the rice paddies, vanishing from sight in the blink of an eye.
Once those two lively voices were gone, a sudden silence settled over everything.
Just that alone made it feel as if the sun had abruptly dropped and evening had arrived all at once.
My house came into view—the one everyone had taken to calling the "Maebara mansion" on their own.
I'd been too fired up to notice, but in truth, my stomach felt like it was about to burst from overeating.
...Or rather, ominous rumblings were echoing from my gut, like approaching thunder.
Once I became aware of how bad things were, I could feel the toll of all that insanity catching up with my body.
In this condition, I probably wouldn't be able to eat a proper dinner.
I should probably confess to Mom before she starts cooking that I'd already stuffed myself silly.
changeReason: New adds detail about confessing to Mom about having stuffed herself, matching fuller Japanese.

TextENGNew: ...Actually, I was probably going to make a beeline for the bathroom the second I got home.
...Ugh, the gurgling and churning sounds coming from my stomach were anything but gentle.
And then we arrived at the spot where Rena and I met every morning and parted ways every evening.
``Today was seriously amazing.``
``Mm.@k@vS09/02/170200166. It really was.``
``...Hm? Are you feeling okay, Keiichi-kun?``
``Nahahaha...@k@vS09/01/170100154. Sorry, I think my stomach's giving out on me.@k@vS09/01/170100155. I'm going to head home and take it easy.``
``Ahahahaha, sounds like your stomach still needs more training, huh?``
``...You ate just as much as I did, you know?@k@vS09/01/170100157. Where does it all go in that tiny body of yours?``
``Ufufufu, that's, well... a girl's secret!``
Rena giggled and evaded the question.
If I pressed any further, she'd probably just punch me to change the subject.
And if she punched me right now, my insides might actually come spilling out...@k So I decided to leave it at that.
``Well, see you, Rena.@k@vS09/01/170100159. See you tomorrow.``
``Mm.@k@vS09/02/170200171. See you tomorrow.``
``You know, I used to live in the city.``
``I never felt like every single day was this enjoyable before.@k@vS09/01/170100162. ...But since moving to Hinamizawa...@k@vS09/01/170100163. ...I think I'm genuinely happy.``
``I feel the same way.@k@vS09/02/170200174. You know, I moved to Hinamizawa a year ago too.@k@vS09/02/170200175. I still can't believe I get to spend such fun, happy days like these.``
``I see.@k@vS09/01/170100165. Then tomorrow's bound to be just as great.``
``That's right.@k@vS09/02/170200177. Yesterday and today were both so much fun.``
``Tomorrow will definitely be wonderful too.``
``When every day is this much fun, it's almost scary—like I might trip and fall one of these days, ahahaha.``
``...When you're going to fall, you fall—no matter how careful you are.``
``That's why the right answer is to enjoy the present with everything you've got, so you're ready whenever that fall comes.``
``You're right.@k@vS09/01/170100168. Living every moment to the fullest—that's the way to go!``

``Well then, goodbye, Keiichi-kun.@k@vS09/02/170200182. Say hi to your mom and dad for me.@k@vS09/02/170200183. ...I've still got energy to burn, hau!@k@vS09/02/170200184. I think I'll go treasure hunting at the mountain of treasures!``
``You're still not satisfied?!@k@vS09/01/170100170. You really are something... I can't compete.``
``What about you?@k@vS09/02/170200186. Want to come along?``
``There's a new pile that I haven't excavated at all yet!``
``I might find something really kyute!``
``Sorry, I'll have to pass.@k@vS09/01/170100172. Besides, those clouds are looking pretty heavy.@k@vS09/01/170100173. Maybe you should just head straight home too, Rena.``
``Hau, on wonderful days like this, I just know I'll find something kyute.@k@vS09/02/170200190. So I'm going to stop by, even if only for a bit.@k@vS09/02/170200191. Ehehehe...☆``
It was only because my stomach was upset that I didn't have the energy to go along.@k If I'd been feeling fine, I would have gone treasure hunting with Rena for sure.
Just like me, Rena was still basking in the afterglow of such a fun day and wasn't ready to call it quits just yet.
``All right, fine.@k@vS09/01/170100175. See ya!@k@vS09/01/170100176. If you find something kyute, you've gotta show me!``
Keiichi-kun waved back again and again, as if reluctant to part.
Then, just like that, he was gone from sight.
Well then, I should get going too. Off to treasure hunting.
I love kyute things, after all.
It's been such a wonderful day—I'm sure something good will happen.
I'm bound to find some wonderful treasure I don't normally come across.
I pedaled hard.
My house was right there, but I didn't go that way. I headed for the road leading down to the dam construction site.
But maybe... maybe it would be okay to go home after all.
But I didn't want to go back to check, only to risk being seen.
After all, today had been so wonderfully fun. I wanted to hold on to this happy feeling until the very last moment.
It was evening, but the darkness seemed to be coming on earlier than usual.
Just as Keiichi-kun had said, the overcast sky had turned leaden before I'd noticed.
One clap of thunder was all it would take to bring on a violent downpour.
changeReason: Old says "It might start raining hard"; new says "One clap of thunder would bring on a violent downpour" -- different causality.

TextENGNew: ``...But that doesn't mean I can just go home.``
Stop it, Rena.
Today was so wonderfully fun.
Let's stay happy right until the very end.
I'm happy.
Every day is so much fun.
Come on, Rena. Say it—the magic words, like always.
Don't think of yourself as foolish.
Actually, maybe fools enjoy life more.
Then I'd gladly choose to be a fool.
``...Hauu~☆@k@vS09/02/170200199. Is there something kyute, I wonder, I wonder?!@k@vS09/02/170200200. Take it hooome~!``
Good, I said it.
I'm sure I'll find something kyute.
As long as I believe something is cute, anything can feel that way.
Come on, let's fill my heart with happy memories of today and yesterday.
And let's dream even bigger about the fun times waiting tomorrow and the day after.
Before I knew it, I'd arrived at the junk heaps by the dam construction site.
I parked my bicycle and made my way down the slope of scrap and bulky refuse.
This place is my castle.
It's almost unbelievable how nobody ever visits this trash heap.
I've spent ages here over a long stretch of time, and hardly anyone has ever passed by.
This truly is the village's blind spot.
I'd heard it was quite lively during the dam war, but once that ended, it became nothing but ruins.
For the villagers' daily lives, there was simply no reason to pass through here. It had become a forgotten place.
The trucks that dump garbage apparently come in the dead of night. At the very least, I've never actually seen them unloading.
If new piles of trash showed up more often, it would at least give me something to pass the time with.
...But lately, there's been no sign of any new arrivals.
I walked all the way down the slope and circled around to the far side of the finished heap.
No one comes to a place like this.
I've spent a long time here, and nobody has ever disturbed this spot.
Even the younger kids from school won't come near here.
In their world, it's believed that the severed arm from the dam foreman's dismemberment murder is hidden somewhere in these heaps, and they say this place is cursed.
Supposedly, a one-armed ghost still wanders here, searching for its missing arm.
Sadly, I've never encountered such a colorful specter.
I'm not scared.
If anything, I'd like it to appear.
I wouldn't mind helping it search for its missing arm.
And if it were lonely from being all alone, I'd want it to keep me company.
A station wagon lies buried in the scrap heap.
Apart from the missing tires, it's mostly intact.
This car is my secret hideout.
Unfortunately, the sliding door and the rear door won't open.
So it's a bit of a hassle, but I climb in through the passenger seat and make my way to the back.
Of course, I take off my shoes at the passenger seat before going in.
From the outside, it looks like a filthy wreck—but that's only on the outside.
I'd cleaned and tidied up the inside, so it was quite nice.@k It took a lot of work, you know?
I'd folded the back seats down to clear more space.
Then I'd found a mattress at the junk pile and laid it out in the open area.
But it felt a bit wrong as it was, so I brought a sheet from home to cover it.
Add a few of my favorite cushions lined up on top, and voilà—my very own perfect bed was complete.
Cushions and stuffed animals.
And that's not all—look.@k Ehehe, I've got snacks stockpiled here too.
Right now I have a bag of fruit candy in four colors, and—oh, these are no good anymore—I had biscuits, but they've gone soggy.
My bear water bottle is empty right now.
On Sundays when I decide to spend time here, I fill this bottle with sweet tea and bring it along.
I've also set up a modest supply of tissues, a flashlight, spare batteries, and various other necessities.
And to pass the time while I'm here, I've brought two or three paperbacks.@k Lately, I've also been keeping hamsters in a basket I found here, doting on the two little squeaking darlings.
Oh, and here's the most amazing part.@k What do you think this is?@k Look—click.
A soft glow illuminated the hideout. It was a desk lamp.
In a corner of the old construction office ruins, there's an exposed electrical outlet.
I'd assumed there was no way it still had power—but to my surprise,
It actually worked.
changeReason: Old says "Electricity is still being supplied to the outlet"; new says "it actually worked" -- different specificity.

TextENGNew: So when I found a huge pile of discarded extension cords, I thought: what if I connected them all and ran them to my hideout?
Comfortable, stocked with snacks, my favorite books, and even a light.
If I really wanted to, I could probably even spend the night here.
...Though the nights get too cool, so I might catch a cold.
Ahaha.@k Nice little hideout, isn't it?
At first, I'd worried someone might find it and mess things up.@k But to this day, not once has my hideout been tampered with.
Because nobody comes here, and everyone has forgotten this place exists.
That's why this truly is my secret hideout—mine and mine alone.
Fortunately, I'd eaten so much today that I wasn't the least bit hungry.
I could just say I was playing at a friend's house and had dinner there.
So I'd take my time here and relax.
And I'd be as kind to myself as I possibly could.
Yesterday's water gun battle was unbelievably fun.
My heart had been racing during that one-on-one showdown with Keiichi-kun.
And the uproar at Angel Mort today was just as incredible.
I'd never been part of such wild antics with so many people before.
The afterglow of fun still lingered so strongly that I was sure I'd dream about it tonight.
So.@k I wanted to hold on to this feeling of happiness, if only a little longer.
So I'd be kind to myself here.
I'd take off these cramped shoes and stretch my toes out wide.
And I'd cherish this time when I could just be myself.
``Mmmmmmm...mm.``
Once I'd taken my shoes off and settled in, the thought of putting them back on felt impossibly bothersome.
I'd been running around all day and was exhausted.@k The treasure hunt could wait.
As the twilight deepened, the desk lamp's glow grew more reassuring, casting ever-darker shadows.
It really was getting dark faster than usual.
...Just as I thought that, the sporadic patter of raindrops on the roof began.
...Patter.@k ...Pitter-patter... pitter-patter-patter.
No sooner had the rhythm quickened than distant thunder rolled in, and with it came a torrential downpour.
No matter how hard it poured, it was a different world inside here.
changeReason: Old adds emotional reaction ("making me feel like"); new says "it was a different world inside here" -- different focus.

TextENGNew: This is my castle, after all.
Feeling slightly chilled, I unfolded a large blanket and wrapped myself in it.
Here, alone, in a place nobody knows about, surrounded by my own familiar scent, all snuggled up like this...@k Even being stranded alone in a downpour brought a strange sense of peace.
If anything, the rain itself felt dear to me—as though it were keeping intruders away from my castle.
Small happinesses fill this place.
My own personal favorites, everywhere.
That's why I'm happy when I'm here.
...Layering on one more blanket, it became as warm as being under a proper futon.
I was gradually lulled toward drowsiness by the pleasant percussion of raindrops striking the scrap metal heaped all around.
This is a castle of junk.
These cast-off things were no longer needed by anyone, so they gathered here.
This is their kingdom.
A kingdom of things that nobody wants.
The feeling that I belonged here mixed together with the knowledge that this was merely a temporary shelter—@k...that I had a place I was supposed to return to.
...No. I shouldn't think about things like that.
Because once I realize this isn't really where I belong,
...I remember that I'm sitting in the middle of a filthy, unsanitary trash heap...
Let me sink a little deeper into this drowsiness.
...Thinking is poison.
I wanted to keep the kind Rena inside me safe—to shield her from that poison so she could stay pure and innocent forever.
A little longer.@k Just a little longer... let me drift deeper into this drowsiness...
